mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: hepatosplenomegaly, neurological symptoms (myopathy, axonal degeneration, somatic sensory dysfunction)
renal involvement (glycosuria), cardiovascular issues (hypertension, heart valve anomaly - aortopulmonary window), and peripheral neuropathies
sclerodactyly, Raynaud's phenomenon, pulmonary artery hypertension, kidney problems (nephrogenic rest), and possible gastrointestinal manifestations (abdominal pain, difficulty swallowing)
splenomegaly, yellowish skin discoloration (xanthoma), and lipid storage disorder
café au lait spots, neurofibromas, iris hamartomas (Lisch nodules), scoliosis, and possibly Frengel's sign or optic nerve glioma
coarse facial features, short stature, corneal cloudiness, hearing loss, and progressive motor deterioration
myopathy with proximal muscle weakness, respiratory insufficiency, and enlarged liver and spleen
skeletal deformities such as kyphoscoliosis, joint stiffness, and short stature; also includes corneal opacification and dental crowding
demyelination leading to motor deficiencies, visual disturbances, and seizures
early-onset obesity, sensorineural hearing impairments, retinitis pigmentosa, diabetes mellitus, and dilated cardiomyopathy
